prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: PEVA films with 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content were cured at 150 C under vacuum and subjected to damp heat at 85C/85%RH per IEC 61215 for 1000 and 200
2. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: Tensile strength, thermal stability and crosslinking were greater for PEVA with lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for higher VA (33 and 40%
3. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: Results recommend reducing VA content in PEVA to 24% for better encapsulation response against damp heat aging
4. [SILVER] Source: Effect of Thermal Aging on the Tensile Bond Strength at Redu | Statement: Thermal aging via 5,000 thermocycles induced a significant decrease in micro-tensile bond strength values with all seven adhesive systems tested
5. [SILVER] Source: Effect of Thermal Aging on the Tensile Bond Strength at Redu | Statement: One-step self-etch adhesives obtained the lowest MTBS results after both aging treatments with significantly reduced adhesive capacity after thermocyc
